name: Marshmallows
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon vanilla extract (a clear vanilla extract will keep the final product as white as possible)
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter a 9x13-inch baking pan.
2. Combine the powdered sugar and cornstarch in a small bowl. Use one-third of this mixture to coat the pan; reserve the rest.
3. Shake the powder mixture around in the pan so it adheres to both the sides and bottom.
4. In a saucepan, combine the granulated sugar, gelatin, and salt. Stir in the cold water until the gelatin softens (about 5 minutes).
5. Place over low heat; stir constantly until all sugar has dissolved. Do not allow the mixture to boil.
6. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature. Stir in the vanilla extract.
7. Pour the mixture into a large bowl. Using an electric mixer, beat at high speed until thickened and forms soft peaks (about 10-15 minutes).
8. Spread the mixture evenly over the prepared pan; dust with the remaining powdered sugar-cornstarch mixture.
9. Cover loosely with foil and let stand at room temperature for at least two hours or overnight.
10. When set, remove from the pan and cut into 1-inch squares (or desired shape). Dip the knife or cutter in cold water between cuts to prevent sticking.
11. Sprinkle remaining powdered sugar-cornstarch over the marshmallows; store at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 months.
